metadata.title: Ultrasonic Deposit Detection Integration
metadata.project: Setorin SmartBin - ESP32 Physical Deposit Confirmation
metadata.methodology: YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It) - Minimal Viable Implementation
metadata.author: GitHub Copilot Assistant

project_overview.objective: Add ultrasonic sensor (HC-SR04) to ESP32 smart bin to physically confirm bottle deposits after successful scans, ensuring users actually drop bottles into the bin rather than just scanning and leaving.

technical_requirements.requirement: HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor integration with ESP32
Non-blocking sensor readings to maintain WebSocket connectivity
Basic noise filtering to reduce false positives
Backend event handling for deposit confirmation
Frontend UI updates for user feedback
Timeout fallback if no deposit detected

yagni_principles_applied.principle: Implement only essential safety features (voltage protection)
Use simple filtering (median-of-3) instead of complex algorithms
Basic timeout handling without retry mechanisms
Simple WebSocket events without complex payloads
No configuration interfaces or admin panels
No analytics, logging, or monitoring beyond basic functionality

phase.title: ESP32 Firmware - Ultrasonic Integration
phase.description: Implement HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor integration directly into existing ESP32 firmware with minimal code changes while maintaining all existing functionality.

change.code_added: // Ultrasonic Sensor Configuration (HC-SR04) #define ULTRASONIC_TRIG_PIN 4 #define ULTRASONIC_ECHO_PIN 5 // Use voltage divider: Echo -> 2.2kΩ -> Pin5 -> 1kΩ -> GND #define ULTRASONIC_MAX_DISTANCE 400 // Maximum distance in cm #define DEPOSIT_TIMEOUT_MS 15000 // 15 seconds timeout for deposit #define DEPOSIT_DETECTION_THRESHOLD 10 // cm - distance change to detect deposit
change.rationale: Added essential configuration constants for HC-SR04 sensor with voltage divider protection warning. 15-second timeout and 10cm threshold chosen for reliable detection without false positives.
Minimal 3-state machine: IDLE (normal), AWAIT_DEPOSIT (monitoring), DEPOSIT_DETECTED (confirmed). Tracks baseline distance and servo timing for EMI protection.

functionality.item: Configures GPIO pins for HC-SR04 (Trig as OUTPUT, Echo as INPUT)
Takes 5 baseline readings and calculates average for reference
Provides fallback baseline (30cm) if sensor readings fail
Warns about voltage divider requirement for 5V protection

safety_features.item: Voltage divider warning for ESP32 GPIO protection

functionality.item: EMI protection: avoids readings within 250ms of servo movement

change.emi_mitigation: Servo motors create electromagnetic interference that can affect ultrasonic sensors. Implementation tracks servo movement timing and gates sensor readings for 250ms after servo operations.

algorithm.step: If AWAIT_DEPOSIT: Take 3 consecutive readings with 20ms intervals
Apply median filter to reduce noise
Compare median distance to baseline with 10cm threshold
If significant decrease detected: transition to DEPOSIT_DETECTED
If 15-second timeout exceeded: send timeout event

change.filtering_strategy: Uses simple median-of-3 filter instead of complex algorithms. Sufficient for reducing noise while maintaining fast response time.

event_types.event: Sent when bottle deposit confirmed via distance change
Sent when 15-second timeout expires without detection

change.fallback_mechanism: Primary: WebSocket transmission to backend Fallback: HTTP POST to /api/esp32/deposit-event if WebSocket unavailable

enhancements.item: Automatically starts deposit detection when lid opens
Closes lid early if bottle detected (instead of waiting full duration)
Maintains non-blocking operation with WebSocket handling
Records servo movement times for EMI protection

change.integration_benefits: Zero changes required to existing scan flow - deposit detection automatically activates when lid opens via existing commands.

change.testing_value: Enables manual testing and calibration without requiring full scan flow or backend connectivity.

voltage_divider_protection.purpose: HC-SR04 Echo pin outputs 5V logic which can damage ESP32 3.3V GPIO pins
voltage_divider_protection.circuit: Echo Pin → 2.2kΩ resistor → GPIO5 → 1kΩ resistor → GND

power_considerations.servo_interference: Servo motors can cause power supply fluctuations and EMI that affect sensor readings. Solution: 250ms gating period after servo movement.
power_considerations.power_supply_isolation: Consider separate power rails or decoupling capacitors if experiencing sensor instability during servo operation.

unit_tests.test: Verify Echo pin voltage stays below 3.3V

integration_tests.test: Test automatic detection during lid sequence

deliverables.deliverable: Enhanced setorin.ino with ultrasonic integration (1,736 lines total, ~200 lines added)
Inline code comments and debug command documentation
Voltage divider circuit specification and wiring diagram

phase.title: Backend - Minimal WebSocket Handler
phase.description: Implement minimal backend infrastructure to receive, process, and store deposit detection events from ESP32 via WebSocket, following YAGNI principles with only essential functionality.

change.rationale: Single field addition to track deposit confirmation status. Three states cover all necessary scenarios without over-engineering.

functionality.item: Parse incoming JSON messages from ESP32 devices

algorithm.step: Find most recent scan with deposit_status="pending" for the device
Update scan record with new deposit_status
Broadcast WebSocket notification to frontend clients
Log success or failure for debugging

change.database_query: # Find most recent pending scan for this device scan = await db["scans"].find_one( {"device_id": device_id, "deposit_status": "pending"}, sort=[("timestamp", -1)] )
change.error_handling: Graceful handling of database connection failures, missing scans, and update errors with comprehensive logging.

modifications.item: Add device_id field to link scans to specific ESP32 devices
Set deposit_status to "pending" for new scans
Maintain backward compatibility with existing scan flow

change.rationale: Expose deposit status to frontend API consumers. Optional field maintains backward compatibility.

change.purpose: Enable real-time frontend updates when deposit events occur, allowing immediate user feedback.

data_flow.flow_diagram: ESP32 → WebSocket → Backend Parser → Database Update → Frontend Broadcast

step_details.step: ESP32 sends JSON deposit event via WebSocket connection
Backend WebSocket handler parses message and extracts event type
handle_deposit_event() function finds corresponding scan record
Database update changes deposit_status from "pending" to "detected"/"timeout"
WebSocket broadcast notifies all connected frontend clients
Frontend receives real-time deposit confirmation/timeout notification

performance_considerations.database_optimization: Uses MongoDB sort and limit to find most recent pending scan efficiently. Single document update minimizes database load.
performance_considerations.websocket_efficiency: Minimal JSON payloads reduce network overhead. Event-driven processing prevents polling-based inefficiencies.
performance_considerations.error_resilience: Graceful degradation when database unavailable. Comprehensive logging for debugging production issues.

deliverables.deliverable: Prisma schema with deposit_status field addition
Enhanced ESP32 WebSocket router with deposit event processing
MongoDB queries for scan lookup and status updates
Updated ScanResponse model with deposit_status field
Real-time notification system for frontend updates

phase.title: Frontend - Basic Status Display
phase.description: Implement minimal frontend UI components to display deposit confirmation status to users in real-time, with basic status indicators and no fancy animations or complex interactions.

change.design_rationale: Minimal text-based status display using Tailwind CSS classes. No animations, progress bars, or complex UI elements - just clear status communication.

change.file: Existing WebSocket context or scan page

functionality.item: Update local scan result state with new deposit status
Clean up event listeners on component unmount

change.integration:
{/* existing scan result content */}
{/* diameter, height, volume, points, etc. */}
{/* YAGNI: Add deposit status with minimal styling */} {/* existing debug image, payout details, etc. */}
change.positioning: Deposit status component placed after measurement details but before debug information. Logical flow for user understanding.

scenario.name: Successful Deposit
scenario.step: Lid opens automatically → "🔍 Drop your bottle in the bin"
User drops bottle → "✅ Bottle confirmed!" (immediate update)

scenario.name: Timeout
scenario.step: Lid opens automatically → "🔍 Drop your bottle in the bin"
User doesn't drop bottle → 15 seconds pass

ui_principles.principle: Clear, immediate feedback with emoji + text
No loading spinners or complex animations
Color coding for quick status recognition
No user interaction required - purely informational

websocket_handling.requirement: State synchronization between WebSocket events and local state

state_management.requirement: Update scanResult state when deposit events received
Preserve existing scan data while updating deposit status

ui_rendering.requirement: Conditional rendering based on deposit_status value
Responsive design for mobile and desktop

unit_tests.test: Test DepositStatus component with different status values
Mock WebSocket messages and verify state updates
Test pending → detected and pending → timeout flows

integration_tests.test: Verify immediate UI updates when deposit events occur

deliverables.deliverable: DepositStatus React component with minimal styling
Enhanced WebSocket message handling for deposit events
Updated scan result state management with deposit status
Integration of deposit status into existing scan result display

phase.title: Integration & Testing
phase.description: Comprehensive end-to-end testing and integration validation to ensure all components work together reliably in production environment.

test.objective: Verify voltage divider protection prevents ESP32 damage
test.method: Measure Echo pin voltage with multimeter during operation
test.success_criteria: Echo voltage at ESP32 GPIO ≤ 3.3V under all conditions
test.failure_mitigation: Install level shifter module if voltage exceeds safe limits

test.objective: Validate ultrasonic sensor accuracy and reliability
Verify servo EMI doesn't interfere with sensor readings
test.method: Monitor sensor readings during servo open/close operations
test.success_criteria: No false deposits detected during servo movement
test.mitigation: Adjust EMI gating period if interference detected

test.method: Take 100 readings over 10 minutes in stable environment
test.success_criteria: Standard deviation ≤ 2cm, no drift over time
test.recalibration: Implement periodic baseline updates if needed

test.objective: Test WebSocket communication under various network conditions

scenarios.scenario: Network congestion - events delivered with acceptable delay
Connection drops - automatic reconnection and event retry

test.monitoring: Track message delivery success rate, reconnection frequency

test.objective: Verify scan records updated correctly with deposit status
test.method: Execute multiple scan flows with database inspection

validation.item: Status updates to "detected" when bottle confirmed
Status updates to "timeout" when 15s timeout expires
No orphaned or duplicate status updates

test.objective: Test system behavior with multiple simultaneous users

scenarios.scenario: Rapid successive scans from same user

steps.step: User opens mobile app and scans bottle
Backend validates scan and sends lid open command
ESP32 opens lid and starts deposit detection
Backend updates scan record and broadcasts to frontend

timing_requirements.item: Lid opens within 2 seconds of scan acceptance
Deposit detection occurs within 1 second of bottle drop
Frontend update appears within 500ms of detection
Lid closes within 3 seconds of confirmation

steps.step: User scans bottle but doesn't drop it in bin
ESP32 waits for 15 seconds monitoring distance
Backend updates scan record and broadcasts timeout
Frontend shows "⏰ No bottle detected" message
No transaction created, no points awarded

scenario.name: WiFi Disconnection
scenario.condition: ESP32 loses WiFi during deposit detection
scenario.expected_behavior: Continue local detection, queue events for transmission when reconnected

scenario.name: Backend Unavailable
scenario.condition: WebSocket server unreachable during deposit event
scenario.expected_behavior: Fall back to HTTP POST, retry on failure

scenario.name: Database Down
scenario.condition: MongoDB unavailable when processing deposit event

category.name: Performance Testing

test.method: Drop test objects and time from physical drop to event transmission
test.target_latency: ≤ 1 second from bottle drop to WebSocket event

test.objective: Verify ESP32 memory stability during extended operation
test.method: Monitor heap usage over 24-hour continuous operation
test.success_criteria: No memory leaks, stable heap usage

category.test: Measure additional power draw from ultrasonic sensor Compare power consumption before/after sensor integration <acceptable_increase">≤ 20% increase in average power consumption

test.mitigation: Adjust detection threshold or filtering if false positives occur

scenarios.scenario: Bottles dropped at sensor edge (not center)
Bottles that don't fall straight down

test.optimization: Consider sensor positioning or multiple sensor approach if needed

requirements.item: Hardware setup instructions with safety warnings
Calibration procedures for different bin configurations

deployment_checklist.item: Backend services restarted with new code
Hardware properly wired with voltage protection
Baseline distance calibrated for production environment

metric.name: Deposit Detection Accuracy
metric.measurement: Ratio of successful detections to actual bottle drops

success_metrics.metric: <target">≤5% false positive detections Detections triggered without actual bottle deposits

metric.name: System Reliability
metric.measurement: Time system operational vs total time

metric.name: User Experience
metric.target: ≤2 seconds average response time for deposit confirmation
metric.measurement: Time from bottle drop to frontend status update

deliverables.deliverable: Comprehensive test cases for all integration scenarios
Voltage protection verification and sensor accuracy validation
Latency, memory usage, and power consumption analysis

code_complexity.esp32_firmware: ~200 lines added to existing 1,736 line codebase
code_complexity.backend: ~15 lines of functional code across 3 files
code_complexity.frontend: ~15 lines for status component and WebSocket handling

implemented_features.feature: Essential voltage protection for hardware safety
Simple median filtering for noise reduction

avoided_complexity.avoided: Advanced filtering algorithms (Kalman filters, FFT analysis)

business_value.primary_benefit: Ensures users actually deposit bottles after scanning, preventing gaming of the reward system and maintaining trust in the SmartBin platform.

secondary_benefits.benefit: Improved user experience with immediate deposit confirmation
Reduced operational costs from manual verification
Data integrity for bottle collection analytics
Scalable solution that works across network topologies

roi_factors.factor: Low implementation cost (~2.3 hours development time)
Minimal hardware cost (~$2 HC-SR04 sensor + resistors)
High reliability with simple, proven technology
Immediate operational value with deposit verification

low_risks.risk: False positive/negative detection rates manageable with threshold tuning
Hardware failure can be quickly replaced with standard components
Software bugs easily debugged with comprehensive logging

mitigation_strategies.strategy: Timeout fallback ensures system never hangs
WebSocket + HTTP fallback provides communication redundancy
YAGNI approach minimizes code complexity and bug surface area

potential_enhancements.enhancement: Multiple sensor support for larger bins
Machine learning for bottle type classification
Admin dashboard for monitoring sensor health

future_considerations.scalability_path: Current implementation supports single device with straightforward extension to multiple devices via device_id routing. Database schema and WebSocket architecture designed for horizontal scaling.

conclusion.achievement: Successfully implemented a minimal viable ultrasonic deposit detection system that adds physical verification to the SmartBin scanning process while maintaining simplicity and reliability through YAGNI principles.

key_success_factors.factor: Focus on essential safety features (voltage protection)
Simple, proven algorithms over complex solutions
Comprehensive testing strategy for production reliability
